articles.title: Thousands of Google workers demand layoff protections amid AI boom in petition to CEO
articles.summary:
The petition to Sundar Pichai, the CEO, included more than 4,500 signatures and included calls for buyout options
Google workers on Thursday delivered a petition calling for layoff protections as tech giants continue to slash their workforces while pouring billions into AI.
“Make
articles.titleZh: 数千名谷歌员工向首席执行官请愿，要求在人工智能蓬勃发展之际提供裁员保护
articles.summaryZh:
致首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊 (Sundar Pichai) 的请愿书有超过 4,500 个签名，并呼吁提出买断选择
周四，谷歌员工提交了一份请愿书，呼吁在科技巨头继续裁员、同时向人工智能投入数十亿美元之际提供裁员保护。
“制作

contentParagraphs.en: Google workers on Thursday delivered a petition calling for layoff protections as tech giants continue to slash their workforces while pouring billions into AI.“Make no mistake: this is a company that is enjoying massive, unprecedented success,” Parul Koul, Google software engineer and Alphabet Workers Union president, said outside the company’s California headquarters after delivering the petition to the office of the CEO, Sundar Pichai’. Koul pointed to Google’s $4tn valuation, which has quadrupled over the last six years: “These layoffs and cuts are not difficult decisions, but simply profit being put over the people that make this company run.”The petition, which was led by the union and includes more than 4,500 signatures, calls for guaranteed severance, buyouts before mandatory layoffs in all product areas and the option to take severance as extended paid leave. Union members are also asking to end performance ratings they say are based on achieving quotas rather than merit.Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment.Upon delivering the petition, workers were “greeted with closed doors and no response for the most part”, Koul said. They left the petition with a staff member in Pichai’s office, who committed to delivering it to the CEO, she added. “This petition is the largest piece of employee feedback that Google has received about job security,” she said.At Thursday’s press conference, workers called out Alphabet’s mass layoffs in 2023, which drew shouts of “shame” from the crowd. Workers also chanted: “Google, Google you can’t hide, we can see your greedy side.”The petition comes as big tech companies thin their ranks, with some explicitly citing AI as a reason.Google has been cutting its workforce in recent months as it ramps up its AI spend. Google Cloud quietly laid off some employees about two months ago, according to Business Insider. And last summer, the company eliminated more than one-third of its managers overseeing small teams, according to an audio recording obtained by CNBC. In the company’s latest earnings call, Anat Ashkenazi ,Alphabet’s chief financial officer, called AI a “key investment area”, in which the company plans to continue hiring and boost marketing support.Google did not comment on whether AI has played a role in its layoffs, but the CEO of the company’s DeepMind division previously told Wired that companies trying to replace developers with AI “have a lack of imagination”.Employees across Silicon Valley are attempting to shield themselves from the effects of the AI boom, whether in their companies’ hiring and firing or spending. The day before the petition went public, dozens of Meta employees sued the social media giant for allegedly using artificial intelligence tools to tag workers for mass layoffs. Meta, the parent company of Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp, conducted a workforce reduction of about 8,000 employees earlier this year. The workers allege that those AI tools targeted them after they asked for protected or maternity leave or disability accommodation.Meta disputed the allegations; a spokesperson wrote in a statement, “These claims lack merit and are not based on facts.”skip past newsletter promotionafter newsletter promotionMeanwhile, Oracle laid off about 21,000 employees over the last year, and suggested in its latest annual report the adoption and deployment of AI “may continue to result in reductions to our workforce”. Block laid off almost half of its workforce – roughly 4,000 employees – earlier this year, with its CEO, Jack Dorsey, citing efficiency gains due to AI. This month, Microsoft announced plans to cut about 2.1% of its workforce – about 4,800 jobs, mostly in its Xbox gaming division – as it invests in AI.Thursday’s petition builds on the Alphabet union campaign that has already secured at least one victory: voluntary exit packages for more than 70,000 workers. The petition aims to address unmet demands. This isn’t the first time workers have tried to convey them to executives, Koul said.“We’ve organized actions mobilizing hundreds of Googlers around the country to raise visibility and attention to these concerns and despite this, Google management has chosen to ignore us,” she said. “This is why we gathered here in person today.”Dan Freedman, Google software engineer and Alphabet union member who works on AI tools for designers, was among several employees at the press conference who raised concerns about AI’s impact on workers’ jobs. After AI was added to his job requirements, he feared he wasn’t using it enough and that it could replace him. He recoils with anxiety when he hears about layoffs at the company. “I have to wonder if I’m next,” he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周四，随着科技巨头继续裁员，同时向人工智能领域投入数十亿美元，谷歌员工提交了一份请愿书，呼吁提供裁员保护。“毫无疑问，这是一家正在享受巨大、前所未有的成功的公司，”谷歌软件工程师兼 Alphabet 工人工会主席帕鲁尔·库尔 (Parul Koul) 在将请愿书递交给首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊 (Sundar Pichai) 办公室后，在公司加州总部外表示。库尔指出，谷歌的估值为 4 兆美元，在过去六年中翻了两番：“这些​​裁员和削减并不是困难的决定，而只是将利润放在让这家公司运营的员工身上。”请愿书由工会牵头，有超过 4,500 名签名，呼吁保证遣散费，在所有产品领域强制裁员之前进行买断，并可以选择将遣散费作为延长带薪休假。工会成员还要求终止他们所说的基于完成配额而不是绩效的绩效评级。谷歌没有立即回应置评请求。科尔说，在递交请愿书后，工人们“受到了关门的欢迎，而且大部分都没有得到回应”。她补充说，他们把请愿书留给了皮查伊办公室的一名工作人员，后者承诺将其交给首席执行官。她说：“这份请愿书是谷歌收到的关于工作保障的最大一份员工反馈。”在周四的新闻发布会上，员工们对 Alphabet 2023 年的大规模裁员表示不满，引发了人群的“耻辱”呼声。工人们还高呼：“谷歌，谷歌，你无法隐藏，我们可以看到你贪婪的一面。”这份请愿书是在大型科技公司精简人员队伍之际提出的，其中一些公司明确引用人工智能作为原因。近几个月来，随着人工智能支出的增加，谷歌一直在裁员。据 Business Insider 报道，谷歌云大约两个月前悄悄解雇了一些员工。根据 CNBC 获得的一段录音，去年夏天，该公司裁掉了三分之一以上负责小团队管理的经理。在该公司最新的财报电话会议上，Alphabet 首席财务官阿纳特·阿什肯纳齐 (Anat Ashkenazi) 将人工智能称为“关键投资领域”，该公司计划在该领域继续招聘人员并加强营销支持。谷歌没有评论人工智能是否在其裁员中发挥了作用，但该公司 DeepMind 部门的首席执行官此前告诉《连线》，试图用人工智能取代开发人员的公司“缺乏想象力”。硅谷各地的员工都在试图保护自己免受人工智能热潮的影响，无论是在公司的招聘和解雇或支出。请愿书公开的前一天，数十名 Meta 员工起诉这家社交媒体巨头，称其涉嫌使用人工智能工具标记工人进行大规模裁员。 Facebook、Instagram 和 WhatsApp 的母公司 Meta 今年早些时候裁员约 8,000 名。工人们声称，这些人工智能工具是在他们要求受保护或产假或残疾住宿后针对他们的。Meta 对这些指控提出异议；一位发言人在一份声明中写道，“这些说法缺乏根据，也没有事实依据。” 与此同时，甲骨文在去年解雇了约 21,000 名员工，并在其最新的年度报告中表示，人工智能的采用和部署“可能会继续导致我们的员工人数减少”。今年早些时候，Block 解雇了近一半员工（约 4,000 名员工），其首席执行官 Jack Dorsey 表示，人工智能带来了效率提升。本月，微软宣布计划裁员约 2.1%（约 4,800 个工作岗位，其中大部分是在 Xbox 游戏部门），因为它投资于人工智能。周四的请愿书建立在 Alphabet 工会运动的基础上，该运动已经取得了至少一项胜利：为超过 70,000 名员工提供自愿离职方案。请愿书旨在解决未满足的需求。科尔说，这并不是工人们第一次试图将这些问题传达给高管。“我们已经组织了行动，动员全国数百名谷歌员工来提高人们对这些问题的关注和关注，尽管如此，谷歌管理层却选择忽视我们，”她说。 “这就是我们今天亲自聚集在这里的原因。” 谷歌软件工程师、Alphabet 工会成员丹·弗里德曼 (Dan Freedman) 为设计师开发人工智能工具，他是出席新闻发布会的几位员工之一，他对人工智能对工人工作的影响表示担忧。当人工智能被添加到他的工作要求中后，他担心自己没有充分使用它，并且它可能会取代他。当他听说公司裁员时，他焦虑地退缩了。 “我不得不想知道我是否是下一个，”他说。

articles.title: I wouldn't marry him until he paid off his debt, now I'm in charge of our money
articles.summary: Sarah and her husband have shared one account for 25 years, but she says managing it falls to her.
articles.titleZh: 在他还清债务之前我不会嫁给他，现在我负责我们的钱
articles.summaryZh: 莎拉和她的丈夫共用一个账户已有 25 年，但她说管理这个账户的责任落在了她的身上。

contentParagraphs.en: When Sarah Reeve got engaged she gave fiance Lee an ultimatum: he had to pay off his debt before she would marry him."I was paying my mortgage and bills whereas he was giving his mum some rent," Sarah says of their situations when they met in their early 20s. "I told him I wouldn't marry him if he had any debts," says the 45-year-old.So they set a wedding date for two years ahead which gave Lee the time to pay off the £2,000 bank loan - £4,000 in today's money, external - he had taken out to buy a car. Once Lee's debt was cleared, the couple paid everything into a joint account and Sarah took charge of bills, saving and budgeting."He said 'you can sort it all out and take charge with money because I'm rubbish with it,'" she says. Sarah's experience reflects a wider trend of more than four fifths of women being actively involved in managing daily finances like day-to-day spending and household budgeting, according to St James's Place's Women and Wealth Report.Sarah earns £24,000 working part-time in insurance and Lee worked in maintenance at the same factory for 27 years, earning about £26,000, before being made redundant four years ago. He now works for himself in property maintenance and earns about £30,000. The couple, who have been together for 25 years and have two daughters, aged 19 and 21, have always thought of money as shared."It's very much our money rather than mine or yours which is really nice especially as I took four years off work when we had children," says Sarah.But being the financially responsible one has not always been easy and Sarah wishes Lee would take more ownership as "I feel like it's all down to me", she says.She says Lee is not money-orientated and is happy for her to make the decisions."He says, 'I met you and I had nothing, so I don't care if I have nothing,'" she says.But Sarah says she felt the pressure of planning for their future.They had "a little pot of money" but "I never knew what to do with it, I didn't have the confidence and I wouldn't even know where to start," she says.St James's Place's research of 6,000 people found only 44% of women feel confident making changes to investments on their own, compared with 63% of men. After her widowed mother saw a financial adviser, Sarah decided to seek advice."I thought deep down that I didn't have enough money to see a financial adviser. In my mind, I thought you needed half a million to do that."The adviser talked through their spending, attitude to risk and future costs, including holidays, a new car and home improvements. Sarah says this made her feel more secure and helped shift their thinking from day-to-day saving to longer-term planning.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当莎拉·里夫订婚时，她给未婚夫李下了最后通牒：他必须在她嫁给他之前还清债务。“我正在支付抵押贷款和账单，而他正在给他妈妈一些房租，”莎拉谈到他们在 20 岁出头相遇时的情况。这位 45 岁的男子说：“我告诉他，如果他有任何债务，我就不会嫁给他。”于是他们把结婚日期提前了两年，这让李有时间还清 2,000 英镑的银行贷款——按今天的货币计算，相当于 4,000 英镑——他用来买一辆汽车。一旦李的债务还清，这对夫妇就将所有费用存入一个联名账户，莎拉负责账单、储蓄和预算。“他说，‘你可以把这一切都解决好，然后用钱来负责，因为我对这件事一窍不通，’”她说。根据圣詹姆斯广场的女性与财富报告，莎拉的经历反映了一个更广泛的趋势，即超过五分之四的女性积极参与管理日常财务，如日常支出和家庭预算。莎拉在保险业做兼职，收入为 24,000 英镑，李在同一家工厂从事维护工作 27 年，收入约为 26,000 英镑，四年前被裁员。他现在为自己从事房产维护工作，收入约为 30,000 英镑。这对夫妇已经在一起 25 年了，有两个女儿，分别为 19 岁和 21 岁，他们一直认为钱是共享的。莎拉说：“这是我们的钱，而不是我的或你的，这真的很好，尤其是当我们有了孩子时，我请了四年的假。”但成为经济上负责任的人并不总是那么容易，莎拉希望李能拥有更多的所有权，因为“我觉得这一切都取决于我”。她说，李不是以金钱为导向的，而且她很高兴她能做出决定。“他说，‘我遇见了你，我一无所有，所以我不在乎我是否一无所有。’”她说。但莎拉说，她感受到了规划未来的压力。他们有“一小笔钱”，但“我从来不知道该怎么用它，我没有信心，我什至不知道从哪里开始，”她说。圣詹姆斯广场对 6,000 人进行的研究发现，只有 44% 的人是女性与 63% 的男性相比，他们对自己改变投资充满信心。在她丧偶的母亲看过财务顾问后，莎拉决定寻求建议。“我内心深处想，我没有足够的钱去看财务顾问。在我看来，我认为你需要五十万才能做到这一点。”顾问谈到了他们的支出、对风险的态度和未来的成本，包括假期、新车和房屋装修。莎拉说，这让她感觉更安全，并帮助他们将思维从日常储蓄转向长期规划。

articles.title: Watch: Flood waters surge in Texas after huge rainstorms
articles.summary: The deluge prompted evacuations and water rescues in the same region where 25 children and two counsellors at Camp Mystic died in flooding in 2025.
articles.titleZh: 观看：特大暴雨后德克萨斯州洪水激增
articles.summaryZh: 2025 年，米斯蒂克营 (Camp Mystic) 的 25 名儿童和两名辅导员在洪水中丧生，洪水促使同一地区进行了疏散和水上救援。

contentParagraphs.en: Severe flooding has struck parts of Texas following days of heavy rainfall, prompting evacuations and water rescues across the region. The storms have impacted several counties near the Mexico border, with the Guadalupe River rising more than 25 feet in just one hour. Residents have been urged to shelter in place, while thousands of people have been left without power. As of now, there have been no immediate reports of deaths or injuries. More than 100 people were killed last year when the Guadalupe River flooded over the July Fourth holiday. That death toll included 25 children, two counsellors and the owner of Camp Mystic, a Texas all-girls camp near Kerrville, Texas.
contentParagraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

articles.title: Republican campaigns see immigration as a winning issue for midterms
articles.summary: Republicans are spending more money and running more ads on immigration than Democrats are ahead of the November midterm elections, according to an NPR analysis of advertisement data.
articles.titleZh: 共和党竞选团队将移民视为中期选举的制胜议题
articles.summaryZh: 根据美国国家公共广播电台 (NPR) 对广告数据的分析，在 11 月中期选举之前，共和党在移民问题上投入的资金和投放的广告比民主党更多。

contentParagraphs.en: Children play on the Mexico-U.S. border wall in Playas de Tijuana, Mexico, on May 10. Border security has been a big theme for Republican ads during the 2026 midterm campaign.
contentParagraphs.zh: 孩子们在墨西哥-美国航线上玩耍5 月 10 日，墨西哥普拉亚斯蒂华纳的边境墙。边境安全一直是 2026 年中期竞选期间共和党广告的一大主题。

contentParagraphs.en: Republicans are leaning into immigration enforcement as one of their top campaign issues this midterm cycle — despite a rocky start to the year for messaging on the president's top policy.
contentParagraphs.zh: 共和党人正倾向于将移民执法作为中期选举周期的首要竞选议题之一——尽管今年开局就总统的首要政策而言，情况并不顺利。

contentParagraphs.en: An NPR analysis of advertisement data from the firm AdImpact shows that when it comes to immigration, Republicans are spending more money and running more ads than Democrats are. The data set includes ads purchased from January through June, before immigration enforcement officers shot and killed people in Maine and Texas this month.
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 对 AdImpact 公司广告数据的分析显示，在移民问题上，共和党比民主党花更多的钱，投放更多的广告。该数据集包括从 1 月到 6 月购买的广告，此后移民执法人员本月在缅因州和德克萨斯州开枪射杀了人们。

contentParagraphs.en: These political ads offer one indication of where each party sees its momentum going with voters, as candidates across the country gear up for the general election in November. The data suggests Republicans see immigration as a winning issue: Since the start of the year, Republicans and their supporting organizations have run nearly 300 ads nationwide that either include a mention of immigration or are solely about immigration. This compares to 62 ads from Democrats and their supporting organizations.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随着全国各地的候选人为 11 月份的大选做准备，这些政治广告提供了一个迹象，表明每个政党如何看待自己的选民势头。数据表明，共和党人将移民视为一个获胜的问题：自今年年初以来，共和党人和他们的支持组织已在全国范围内投放了近 300 个广告，其中要么提及移民，要么仅与移民有关。相比之下，民主党及其支持组织发布了 62 个广告。

contentParagraphs.en: "Republicans stood up for Americans. Democrats sat down for illegals. Thomas Massie sides with these radical-left lunatics," reads one ad funded by the MAGA KY PAC, a political action committee that was set up to defeat Republican Rep. Thomas Massie in the primary. The ad cost over $831,000; Massie, a frequent critic of President Trump, went on to lose his race to Trump-endorsed candidate Ed Gallrein.
contentParagraphs.zh: “共和党人为美国人挺身而出。民主党人为非法移民坐下来。托马斯·马西站在这些激进左翼疯子一边，”由 MAGA KY PAC 资助的一则广告如此写道。MAGA KY PAC 是一个政治行动委员会，成立的目的是在初选中击败共和党众议员托马斯·马西。该广告花费超过 831,000 美元；马西经常批评特朗普总统，后来在竞选中输给了特朗普支持的候选人埃德·加莱因(Ed Gallrein)。

contentParagraphs.en: Among the most expensive was a $928,000 ad buy in the Michigan governor's race.
contentParagraphs.zh: 其中最昂贵的是密歇根州州长竞选期间花费 928,000 美元的广告费用。

contentParagraphs.en: "No greater example of waste, fraud, and abuse in Michigan than using our tax dollars to give benefits to illegal immigrants. As governor, I'll be incredibly supportive of ICE coming here and removing these fraudsters," says Republican candidate Perry Johnson, who calls himself a "MAGA Conservative" and has pitched his business approach to running a state.
contentParagraphs.zh: “在密歇根州，没有比利用我们的税款为非法移民提供福利更典型的浪费、欺诈和滥用行为的例子了。作为州长，我将非常支持 ICE 来到这里并铲除这些欺诈者，”共和党候选人佩里·约翰逊 (Perry Johnson) 说道。

contentParagraphs.en: Immigration was a winning issue for Republicans in the 2024 elections, with themes like increasing border security and reducing crime.
contentParagraphs.zh: 移民问题是共和党在 2024 年选举中获胜的议题，其主题包括加强边境安全和减少犯罪。

contentParagraphs.en: "Campaigns are not trying to change minds. They're trying to shape what the election's about. They're trying to energize the voters they already have," said Cameron Shelton, a professor of political economy at Claremont McKenna College. "If Republicans are investing much more heavily in immigration advertising, one interpretation is that they believe immigration is exactly that kind of [mobilizing] issue in today's electorate."
contentParagraphs.zh: 克莱蒙特·麦肯纳学院政治经济学教授卡梅伦·谢尔顿表示：“竞选活动并不是要改变人们的想法。他们是要塑造选举的内容。他们要激励现有选民。” “如果共和党人在移民广告上投入更多，一种解释是他们认为移民正是当今选民中的那种[动员]问题。”

contentParagraphs.en: Immigration and enforcement are among the top issues for both parties
contentParagraphs.zh: 移民和执法是双方最关心的问题之一

contentParagraphs.en: Most of the ads have run during the primary season, which is now more than half over. Since more than 90% of seats up for grabs in gubernatorial, House and Senate races are considered safe for one party or another, the primary campaign has become decisive for many candidates nationwide.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大部分广告都是在初选期间投放的，目前已过半。由于州长、众议院和参议院竞选中超过 90% 的席位被认为对某一党派或另一党派来说是安全的，因此初选对全国许多候选人来说已变得具有决定性意义。

contentParagraphs.en: Some Democrats became more vocal on the issue of immigration at the start of 2026, particularly in states that were seeing intense waves of enforcement. Democrats in New Jersey, Illinois and Minnesota, for example, referenced the administration's tactics in their calls to "abolish ICE," or Immigration and Customs Enforcement, and argued the administration had gone too far.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年初，一些民主党人在移民问题上变得更加直言不讳，尤其是在执法浪潮密集的州。例如，新泽西州、伊利诺伊州和明尼苏达州的民主党人在呼吁“废除移民和海关执法局”时提到了政府的策略，并认为政府做得太过分了。

contentParagraphs.en: The Illinois Future PAC ran two ads, each worth more than $800,000, earlier this year to support Juliana Stratton's stance on abolishing ICE. The current lieutenant governor later won the Illinois Democratic primary for Senate.
contentParagraphs.zh: 今年早些时候，伊利诺伊州未来 PAC 投放了两则广告，每则价值超过 80 万美元，以支持朱莉安娜·斯特拉顿 (Juliana Stratton) 关于废除 ICE 的立场。现任副州长后来赢得了伊利诺伊州民主党参议员初选。

contentParagraphs.en: But months into the year, Democrats have prioritized other topics, often to differentiate themselves from members of their own party, like on healthcare, while Republicans are keeping immigration-related themes on Americans' screens.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但今年几个月来，民主党人优先考虑了其他话题，通常是为了将自己与本党成员区分开来，比如医疗保健问题，而共和党人则在美国人的屏幕上保留与移民相关的主题。

contentParagraphs.en: During the primary season, Shelton said, campaigns are testing out the issues they think might matter through the general election.
contentParagraphs.zh: 谢尔顿说，在初选期间，竞选团队正在测试他们认为在大选中可能重要的问题。

contentParagraphs.en: For both parties, "Donald Trump" is the top subject in TV ad buys, according to data from AdImpact. "Immigration" is the issue with the second-highest spending for Republicans; for Democrats, "ICE" is the third-highest, after "healthcare."
contentParagraphs.zh: AdImpact 的数据显示，对于两党来说，“唐纳德·特朗普”都是电视广告购买的首要主题。 “移民”是共和党支出第二高的问题；对于民主党人来说，“ICE”排名第三，仅次于“医疗保健”。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's a signal to donors, it's a signal to activists, to interest groups, to local candidates. It helps coordinate a lot of the actors that we think of as the party," Shelton said. "That's another reason why some of these early ads are interesting, because they are signals of the direction that is trying to be set out."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这是向捐助者发出的信号，向活动人士、利益团体、当地候选人发出的信号。它有助于协调我们认为是政党的许多参与者，”谢尔顿说。 “这就是为什么这些早期广告很有趣的另一个原因，因为它们是试图设定的方向的信号。”

contentParagraphs.en: Republicans link top issues to immigration
contentParagraphs.zh: 共和党人将首要问题与移民联系起来

contentParagraphs.en: Between January and June, Republicans outspent Democrats on immigration-related political advertising by about $36 million. Republican ads focused on immigration, which total $53 million in spending, have aired across the country in 88 races and 27 states. Ads for Democratic candidates, which total $17 million, have run in 20 races and 11 states, primarily those that have seen increased immigration enforcement action like California, New York and Illinois.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1 月至 6 月期间，共和党在移民相关政治广告上的支出比民主党多出约 3600 万美元。共和党针对移民的广告总共花费了 5300 万美元，已在全国 27 个州的 88 个种族中播出。民主党候选人的广告总计 1700 万美元，已在 11 个州的 20 场竞选中投放，主要是那些移民执法力度加大的州，如加利福尼亚州、纽约州和伊利诺伊州。

contentParagraphs.en: "Republican candidates have a large menu of issues we are on the right side of that are all very popular amongst voters," said Mike Marinella, national press secretary at the National Republican Congressional Committee. He listed the border, crime and the economy as issues that Republican candidates can connect to immigration.
contentParagraphs.zh: 全国共和党国会委员会全国新闻秘书迈克·马里内拉表示：“共和党候选人有很多问题，我们都站在正确的一边，这些问题都非常受选民欢迎。”他将边境、犯罪和经济列为共和党候选人可以与移民联系起来的问题。

contentParagraphs.en: "Immigration intersects with each of them," he said. "The most effective message depends on the district and how those issues are affecting that particular community," he added.
contentParagraphs.zh: “移民与他们每个人都有交叉，”他说。他补充说：“最有效的信息取决于所在地区以及这些问题如何影响该特定社区。”

contentParagraphs.en: Zach Lahn, who won the GOP primary bid for Iowa governor, spent about $475,000 on an ad in May that vowed to ban H-1B visa holders from being hired by Iowa government agencies and universities, linking immigration and economic concerns.
contentParagraphs.zh: 赢得爱荷华州州长共和党初选的扎克·兰 (Zach Lahn) 在 5 月份花费约 475,000 美元制作了一则广告，该广告誓言禁止 H-1B 签证持有者受爱荷华州政府机构和大学雇用，将移民和经济问题联系起来。

contentParagraphs.en: Crossings at the border have plummeted since Trump took office. Marinella said candidates are still keeping the is
contentParagraphs.zh: 自特朗普上任以来，边境口岸数量大幅下降。马里内拉表示候选人仍保留原样

articles.title: Officials probe whether White House teleprompter operator profited off Trump's words
articles.summary: It's the first known instance of officials investigating suspected insider trading on a prediction market from inside the White House.
articles.titleZh: 官员们调查白宫提词器操作员是否从特朗普的言论中获利
articles.summaryZh: 这是已知的第一例官员在白宫内部调查预测市场上涉嫌内幕交易的事件。

contentParagraphs.en: Then-candidate Trump speaking in front of a teleprompter during a campaign rally in Reno, Nevada in 2024.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2024 年在内华达州里诺举行的竞选集会上，当时的候选人特朗普在提词器前发表讲话。

contentParagraphs.en: Federal regulators are in settlement talks with President Trump's longtime teleprompter operator, who is alleged to have made nearly $100,000 on the prediction market site Kalshi, according to two people with knowledge of the probe who were not authorized to speak publicly.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据两位了解调查情况但未获授权公开发言的人士透露，联邦监管机构正在与特朗普总统的长期提词器操作员进行和解谈判，该操作员据称在预测市场网站 Kalshi 上赚取了近 10 万美元。

contentParagraphs.en: He is suspected of profiting off of his access to the president's prepared remarks on a type of betting on Kalshi known as "mention markets," where people wager on words and expressions the president will or will not say during public events.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他被怀疑利用总统准备好的言论来获利，这种言论对卡尔希进行一种被称为“提及市场”的投注，人们对总统在公共活动中会或不会说的词语和表达进行下注。

contentParagraphs.en: Prediction markets allow people to bet on elections, global policy or even what color tie the president will wear. The markets have exploded in growth in recent months, leading to several instances of high-profile insider trading. But this marks the first time someone inside the White House has been investigated for allegedly abusing that access for prediction market profits.
contentParagraphs.zh: 预测市场允许人们对选举、全球政策甚至总统会穿什么颜色的领带进行投注。近几个月来，市场出现爆炸式增长，导致发生了多起备受瞩目的内幕交易事件。但这标志着白宫内部有人因涉嫌滥用预测市场利润而受到调查。

contentParagraphs.en: Gabriel Perez, who has worked for the president since 2016, is now negotiating with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission over his alleged lucrative betting activity on the "mention markets."
contentParagraphs.zh: 自 2016 年以来一直为总统工作的加布里埃尔·佩雷斯 (Gabriel Perez) 目前正在与商品期货交易委员会就其涉嫌在“提及市场”进行利润丰厚的投注活动进行谈判。

contentParagraphs.en: Ahead of Trump's address to the nation later on Thursday, Kalshi traders have already wagered more than $800,000 on whether the president will say words like "Hormuz," or "rigged election," or "fake news."
contentParagraphs.zh: 在特朗普周四晚些时候向全国发表讲话之前，卡尔什交易员已经对总统是否会说出“霍尔木兹”、“操纵选举”或“假新闻”等词语下了超过 80 万美元的赌注。

contentParagraphs.en: In the case of Perez, Kalshi's surveillance systems detected unusual betting on "mention" markets involving the president that did not follow typical behavior, and when the company examined the accounts behind it, investigators saw that he was a federal employee, according to a source with direct knowledge of the inquiry.
contentParagraphs.zh: 就佩雷斯而言，卡尔希的监控系统发现了涉及总统的“提及”市场的异常投注，这些投注不遵循典型行为，据直接了解调查情况的消息人士称，当该公司检查其背后的账户时，调查人员发现他是一名联邦雇员。

contentParagraphs.en: Records show that as a deputy assistant to the president, Perez was paid $175,000 a year. Trump would sometimes make references to him during appearances, like he did during a 2024 campaign stop in Reno, Nevada in 2024.
contentParagraphs.zh: 记录显示，作为总统副助理，佩雷斯的年薪为 175,000 美元。特朗普有时会在露面时提到他，就像他在 2024 年在内华达州里诺举行的 2024 年竞选活动中所做的那样。

contentParagraphs.en: "I have a guy, Gabe, he's excellent. I've had some real bad ones, but I have Gabe, some of the bad ones, they go so fast. I will go and I say, slow the damn thing," Trump said. "No, a good one is really like gold."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我有一个人，加布，他非常出色。我遇到过一些非常糟糕的人，但我有加布，有些糟糕的人，他们走得太快了。我会去，我说，慢点，”特朗普说。 “不，好东西确实如金子一样。”

contentParagraphs.en: Perez is currently on unpaid administrative leave, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said on Thursday, calling the actions "a disgrace."
contentParagraphs.zh: 白宫新闻秘书卡罗琳·莱维特周四表示，佩雷斯目前正在休无薪行政假，称这些行为是“耻辱”。

contentParagraphs.en: In a statement, Kalshi credited its detection tools with catching Perez.
contentParagraphs.zh: 卡尔希在一份声明中将抓获佩雷斯归功于其检测工具。

contentParagraphs.en: "Our surveillance team promptly flagged and referred these trades to the CFTC after an exchange investigation. We have been assisting regulators on this matter and provided evidence we collected, as we do in any referral," said Robert DeNault, who heads enforcement at Kalshi.
contentParagraphs.zh: Kalshi 执法负责人罗伯特·德诺特 (Robert DeNault) 表示：“在交易所调查后，我们的监控团队立即标记了这些交易，并将其转交给美国商品期货交易委员会 (CFTC)。我们一直在协助监管机构处理此事，并提供我们收集的证据，就像我们在任何转介中所做的那样。”

contentParagraphs.en: Kalshi froze about $90,000 of Perez's profits and he has been banned from betting on the site, one of the sources not authorized to speak publicly said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一位未获授权公开发言的消息人士称，卡尔希冻结了佩雷斯约 9 万美元的利润，并禁止他在该网站上投注。

contentParagraphs.en: Perez did not return a request for comment. ABC News first reported on the investigation into Perez.
contentParagraphs.zh: 佩雷斯没有回复置评请求。美国广播公司新闻首先报道了对佩雷斯的调查。

contentParagraphs.en: The president's unpredictable and digressive speaking style can make for highly volatile trading on the site's "mention markets," where the odds of Trump uttering a particular word or phrases fluctuate drastically, with traders trying to forecast what subject he may veer to next.
contentParagraphs.zh: 总统不可预测且离题的讲话风格可能会导致该网站“提及市场”的交易高度波动，特朗普说出特定单词或短语的可能性剧烈波动，交易者试图预测他接下来可能转向的主题。

contentParagraphs.en: Some traders who consider "mention" markets something of a full time job have installed TV antennas to get a tiny fraction-of-a-second advantage during live events.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些认为“提及”市场是一份全职工作的交易者已经安装了电视天线，以便在现场活动中获得微乎其微的优势。

contentParagraphs.en: Using non-public information to win big or manipulate markets on Kalshi is prohibited by the platform's rules. Such behavior could also be criminally prosecuted as wire fraud, commodities fraud and money laundering.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该平台的规则禁止利用非公开信息在 Kalshi 上赢得巨大市场或操纵市场。此类行为还可能作为电信欺诈、商品欺诈和洗钱受到刑事起诉。

contentParagraphs.en: It is not clear if the Department of Justice is examining the case of Perez.
contentParagraphs.zh: 目前尚不清楚司法部是否正在调查佩雷斯的案件。

contentParagraphs.en: In March, White House staff received a memo warning against using nonpublic government information to place bets on Kalshi and its biggest competitor, Polymarket.
contentParagraphs.zh: 今年 3 月，白宫工作人员收到一份备忘录，警告不要利用非公开政府信息对 Kalshi 及其最大竞争对手 Polymarket 进行押注。

contentParagraphs.en: The memo, which was reviewed by NPR, stated that it is a criminal offense for anyone inside the White House to "buy" or "sell" on the sites. Prediction markets offer "yes" or "no" contracts that change in price based on the speculation of bettors. Aides in the White House were told in the memo that misusing government information "is a very serious offence and will not be tolerated."
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国公共广播电台审阅的这份备忘录指出，白宫内部的任何人在这些网站上“购买”或“出售”都是刑事犯罪。预测市场提供“是”或“否”合约，其价格根据投注者的猜测而变化。白宫助手在备忘录中被告知，滥用政府信息“是非常严重的罪行，不会被容忍”。

contentParagraphs.en: In April, federal prosecutors charged a U.S. Army special forces soldier for making $400,000 on Polymarket ahead of the capture of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro.
contentParagraphs.zh: 4 月，联邦检察官指控一名美国陆军特种部队士兵在抓获委内瑞拉领导人尼古拉斯·马杜罗之前在 Polymarket 赚取了 40 万美元。

contentParagraphs.en: The following month, a Google software engineer was charged with using confidential company information to make $1.2 million on Polymarket, for correctly guessing on Google search trends.
contentParagraphs.zh: 接下来的一个月，一名谷歌软件工程师因正确猜测谷歌搜索趋势而被指控利用公司机密信息在 Polymarket 上赚取 120 万美元。

contentParagraphs.en: Others have been under investigation, too, including former Republican congressman George Santos who investigators say pumped up a Kalshi market by claiming he would attend Trump's 2026 State of the Union address, only to cash out on a "no" trade when he skipped it.
contentParagraphs.zh: 其他人也受到了调查，包括前共和党众议员乔治·桑托斯（George Santos），调查人员称，他声称自己将参加特朗普的 2026 年国情咨文演讲，从而刺激了卡尔希市场，但当他缺席演讲时，他却在“不”交易中套现。

articles.title: UK economy returns to growth in May
articles.summary: The modest growth reverses a slight contraction that had been seen the previous month.
articles.titleZh: 英国经济五月恢复增长
articles.summaryZh: 温和的增长扭转了上个月出现的小幅收缩。

contentParagraphs.en: The UK's economy returned to growth in May, but the expansion was modest as businesses were affected by the impact of the Iran war.The economy grew by 0.1%, the Office for National Statistics (ONS) said, driven by expansion in the UK's service sector, although this was offset by falls in the production and construction sectors.May's growth comes after a slight contraction in April, and analysts said the latest figures suggested the economy had weathered the rise in energy prices caused by the conflict in the Middle East better than expected.However, others noted the UK economy remained "fragile" and incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham faced a challenge to boost growth.Over the three months to May, the ONS said the economy grew by 0.7%, external compared with the previous three-month period."The economy recorded robust growth in the three months to May, though the pace eased slightly as the latest two months showed a weaker picture," said Liz McKeown, director of economic statistics at the ONS."Computer programming and advertising led the way, while the often-volatile pharmaceutical industry also performed well," she added.The economy saw a strong start to the year, but growth has faltered in recent months with the conflict in the Middle East having affected some businesses.The Iran war has pushed up oil and fuel prices, and also disrupted supply chains. The ONS said firms in a number of sectors had flagged the conflict as affecting activity, including some manufacturing industries, hospitality firms, travel agencies and entertainment companies.Since hostilities resumed between the US and Iran last week, the price of oil has risen from about $72 a barrel to $84, although it remains well below the peak of around $120 seen earlier this year."Today's data confirm that growth remains fragile," said Fergus Jimenez-England, associate economist at the National Institute of Economic and Social Research."As energy prices climb once more, all eyes are now on the new prime minister to deliver much-needed stability."Yael Selfin, chief economist at KPMG, said the warmer weather and World Cup might have boosted consumer spending into June and July, but this "may not be enough to offset weakness across other parts of the economy"."The recent rise in energy prices, driven by a pick-up in tensions in the Middle East, could pose a risk to the growth outlook, with financial conditions also tightening as a result," Selfin said.The growth in May "is not a bad welcome gift for incoming PM Andy Burnham", said Paul Dales, chief UK economist at Capital Economics."But with higher energy prices still restraining real incomes, he shouldn't get used to it."Responding to the latest figures, a spokesperson for the Treasury said: "We have the right economic plan which has put the UK in a much stronger position than two years ago with the fastest growth in the G7 in the first quarter and the OECD agreeing that we have restored stability."But Conservative shadow chancellor Sir Mel Stride said chancellor Rachel Reeves had "failed" in her attempts to boost growth."Two years of higher taxes have choked the economy, and now Andy Burnham wants even more taxes to pay for more benefits. "Starmer and Reeves may be on their way out but the problem isn't just the chancellor, it's the Labour Party."
contentParagraphs.zh: 英国经济5月份恢复增长，但由于企业受到伊朗战争的影响，扩张幅度不大。英国国家统计局(ONS)表示，在英国服务业扩张的推动下，英国经济增长了0.1%，尽管这被生产和建筑业的下滑所抵消。5月份的增长是在4月份小幅收缩之后出现的，分析师表示，最新数据表明，英国经济比预期更好地抵御了中东冲突导致的能源价格上涨。其他人指出，英国经济仍然“脆弱”，即将上任的首相安迪·伯纳姆面临提振增长的挑战。英国国家统计局表示，截至 5 月份的三个月内，与前三个月相比，外部经济增长了 0.7%。英国国家统计局经济统计主任利兹·麦基翁 (Liz McKeown) 表示，“截至 5 月份的三个月内，经济录得强劲增长，但由于最近两个月的情况较疲弱，经济增速略有放缓。”经常波动的制药行业也表现良好。”她补充道。今年经济开局强劲，但近几个月增长放缓，中东冲突影响了一些企业。伊朗战争推高了石油和燃料价格，也扰乱了供应链。英国国家统计局表示，包括一些制造业、酒店业、旅行社和娱乐公司在内的多个行业的公司都已将冲突标记为影响经济活动。自上周美国和伊朗之间恢复敌对行动以来，石油价格已从每桶约 72 美元上涨至 84 美元，但仍远低于今年早些时候约 120 美元的峰值。“今天的数据证实增长仍然脆弱，”国家经济研究所副经济学家弗格斯·希门尼斯-英格兰表示。毕马威(KPMG)首席经济学家耶尔·塞尔芬(Yael Selfin)表示，气候变暖和世界杯可能会提振6月和7月的消费者支出，但这“可能不足以抵消经济其他领域的疲软”。“随着能源价格再次攀升，所有人的目光都集中在新总理带来急需的稳定上。”近期，受中东紧张局势升级推动的能源价格上涨，可能对增长前景构成风险，金融状况也在收紧。凯投宏观(Capital Economics)首席英国经济学家保罗·戴尔斯(Paul Dales)表示，5月份的经济增长“对即将上任的首相安迪·伯纳姆来说是一个不错的欢迎礼物”。“但由于能源价格上涨仍然限制了实际收入，他不应该习惯这种情况。”针对最新数据，财政部发言人表示：“我们制定了正确的经济计划，使英国处于比两年前更有利的地位，第一季度是七国集团和经合组织中增长最快的国家。”同意我们已经恢复了稳定。”但保守党影子财政大臣梅尔·斯特莱德表示，财政大臣雷切尔·里夫斯提振增长的努力“失败”了。“两年来的高税收已经抑制了经济，现在安迪·伯纳姆希望用更多的税来支付更多的福利。斯塔默和里夫斯可能即将下台，但问题不仅仅是财政大臣，而是工党。”
